pi_items.source: Jitsi Meet Docker documentation and community resources confirm ~/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web is the standard configuration directory for Docker-jitsi-meet deployments, and custom-config.js is the conventional filename for custom overrides.
Official Jitsi Meet configuration documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) lists 'microphone' as a valid toolbar button identifier
Official Jitsi Meet configuration documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) lists 'camera' as a valid toolbar button identifier
Official Jitsi Meet configuration documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) lists 'hangup' as a valid toolbar button identifier
Official Jitsi Meet configuration documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) lists 'tileview' as a valid toolbar button identifier
Official Jitsi Meet configuration documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) lists 'fullscreen' as a valid toolbar button identifier
Official Jitsi Meet configuration documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) lists 'chat' as a valid toolbar button identifier
Official Jitsi Meet configuration documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) lists 'desktop' as a valid toolbar button identifier
Official Jitsi Meet configuration documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) lists 'invite' as a valid toolbar button identifier
Official Jitsi Meet configuration documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) lists 'raisehand' as a valid toolbar button identifier
Official Jitsi Meet configuration documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) lists 'security' as a valid toolbar button identifier
Official Jitsi Meet configuration documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) lists 'videoquality' as a valid toolbar button identifier
Official Jitsi Meet configuration documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) lists 'filmstrip' as a valid toolbar button identifier
Official Jitsi Meet configuration documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) lists 'shortcuts' as a valid toolbar button identifier
Official Jitsi Meet configuration documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) lists 'stats' as a valid toolbar button identifier
Official Jitsi Meet configuration documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) lists 'feedback' as a valid toolbar button identifier

privileged_info_summary: The task configures a Jitsi Meet instance with a restricted 'Kiosk Mode' toolbar. The configuration path /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/custom-config.js is the standard Docker-jitsi-meet configuration directory and file. All toolbar button names are verified as valid Jitsi Meet button identifiers from official documentation: required buttons are microphone, camera, hangup, tileview, and fullscreen; prohibited buttons are chat, desktop, invite, raisehand, security, videoquality, filmstrip, shortcuts, stats, and feedback. The task uses synthetic data in a containerized Jitsi Meet environment with test meeting rooms.
